notes: Turbidity and level arrive as separate lines. A valid turbidity frame refreshes only turbidity freshness and a valid level frame refreshes only level freshness.
Count the clear streak using valid turbidity samples only. Level samples do not increment or reset that streak.
The settling timer uses controller time and can advance on CONTROLLER_INPUT_NONE steps.
With FILTER ON, turbidity drops quickly. With FILTER OFF, turbidity slowly rebounds upward.
The hidden scenarios can inject disturbances while settling or draining. A robust controller must return to filtering instead of draining cloudy liquid.

task.objective: Filter a cloudy tank until it is clearly clean, wait a short settle window, then drain it without draining early or ignoring later disturbances.
interface: Controller inputs are CONTROLLER_INPUT_TURBIDITY, CONTROLLER_INPUT_LEVEL, CONTROLLER_INPUT_SENSOR_TIMEOUT, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_TURBIDITY, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_LEVEL, and CONTROLLER_INPUT_NONE.
Sensor frames use `SENSE TURB <integer>` and `SENSE LEVEL <integer>`.
Actuator frames must be `ACT FILTER ON|OFF` and `ACT DRAIN OPEN|CLOSED`.
policy: On boot, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN CLOSED.
Until both sensors have been seen validly, stay in the safe all-off state.
If LEVEL < 15, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN CLOSED and remain in COMPLETE.
If TURB > 35 and LEVEL >= 40, command FILTER ON and DRAIN CLOSED.
Count only valid TURB inputs toward the clear streak. A valid TURB <= 35 increments the clear streak and a valid TURB > 35 resets it to zero.
When the clear streak reaches 3 while LEVEL >= 40, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN CLOSED and enter SETTLING with a 400 ms timer.
While SETTLING, if a valid TURB > 40 arrives, reset the clear streak and return to FILTERING with FILTER ON and DRAIN CLOSED.
When SETTLING has lasted at least 400 ms, the latest TURB <= 35, and LEVEL >= 40, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN OPEN and enter DRAINING.
While DRAINING, if a valid TURB > 50 arrives and LEVEL >= 40, command FILTER ON and DRAIN CLOSED, reset the clear streak, and return to FILTERING.
On malformed sensor input, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN CLOSED.
On explicit sensor timeout input, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN CLOSED.
If either sensor becomes stale for more than 1000 ms, command FILTER OFF and DRAIN CLOSED.
decision_summary: Missing any fresh valid sensor: safe all-off state
TURB > 35 with sufficient level: FILTER ON, DRAIN CLOSED, state FILTERING
Three consecutive clear turbidity samples: FILTER OFF, DRAIN CLOSED, state SETTLING
Settled for 300 ms with latest TURB <= 35: FILTER OFF, DRAIN OPEN, state DRAINING
LEVEL < 15: FILTER OFF, DRAIN CLOSED, state COMPLETE
state_model: SAFE_IDLE: fail-safe all-off state or waiting for both sensors
FILTERING: actively reducing turbidity with the filter on
SETTLING: filter off, drain closed, waiting for the clear state to hold for 300 ms
DRAINING: drain open while the liquid remains acceptably clear
COMPLETE: drained below the minimum remaining level

constraints: Keep the existing public controller interface intact.
Actuator output must stay within the published FILTER and DRAIN frames.
Do not open the drain before the settling window has completed.
Any malformed or stale sensor input must drive the controller to the safe all-off state.

notes: This task is harder because it requires a counter, a timer, state-dependent resets, and recovery from late disturbances.
The prose packet explicitly defines the clear streak and settle timing so failures point at controller logic, not at missing task details.
